Copper-silver alloys, while offering improved mechanical properties like higher tensile and compressive strengths as well as better edge retention (option C), can have drawbacks such as poorer tarnish and corrosion resistance. Additionally, they typically contain lower levels of mercury than traditional amalgams but might still include some amount due to manufacturing processes.
